title: Today's AI Brief: AI grifters are creating fake Black people to sell Shein junk
summary: AI-generated fake Black people selling Shein products on TikTok, thermal cameras helping ships avoid gray whales, and more.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for AI grifters are creating fake Black people to sell Shein junk
imageCaption: AI grifters are creating fake Black people to sell Shein junk (theverge.com)

body.text: Welcome to Today's AI Brief. I'm Leo Stone, and I'm here to walk you through some of the latest happenings in the world of artificial intelligence. Let's dive right in.

On a much more positive note, thermal cameras and AI are being used to help ships steer clear of gray whales in San Francisco Bay. This technology is a great example of how AI can be used for good, like preventing accidents and protecting wildlife. It's heartening to see AI being used in this way.

What's interesting here is how these two stories highlight the dual nature of AI. On one hand, you have AI being used for nefarious purposes, like grifting. On the other, you have AI being used to make the world a better place, like protecting whales. It's a reminder that AI is just a tool, and its impact depends on how it's used.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
